stemHtml: Which of these correctly describes the operating principle of an ionisation smoke detector?

options.a: Electric charge is only able to flow to the detector when smoke particles are present, which then triggers an alarm.
options.b: A change in current (amperes) is detected when smoke is present, triggering an alarm.
options.c: Ions are scattered by the smoke, and are detected by the detector, causing an alarm.
options.d: The ionisation process of the radioactive material is increased when smoke is present, and this is sensed by a photoelectric detector to trigger an alarm.
